description:
You are an IT-support technician who just received three separate trouble-tickets complaining that some internal services are unreachable.  
    Two configuration files have already been collected from the affected hosts and copied to your workstation.

    1.  Location of the configuration files  
        • /home/user/support_ticket/configs/app1.ini  
        • /home/user/support_ticket/configs/app2.ini  

    2.  File format  
        They are classic INI files that use section headers (e.g., “[AuthService]”) followed by “key=value” pairs.  
        Only three keys may legitimately appear inside each section:  
            enabled – strictly the string “true” or “false” (lower-case)  
            port    – an integer between 1 and 65535  
            notes   – an optional free-text line that should be ignored for this task  

    3.  What you must do  
        a. Parse BOTH files and inspect EVERY section (service).  
        b. Decide whether each service is correctly configured according to the following rules:  
           • If enabled=false  → status is “OK” regardless of the port value.  
           • If enabled=true   → status is “OK” only when:  
                 – a port key is present,  
                 – the port value is between 1024 and 65535 (inclusive), AND  
                 – no other *enabled=true* service in ANY file uses the same port number.  
           • Anything else (missing port, out-of-range port, port collision) → status is “ERROR”.  

    4.  Produce a single audit log at:  
            /home/user/support_ticket/report/service_audit.log  

        4.1 The report MUST be plain-text (UTF-8) with UNIX line endings.  
        4.2 The first line is a constant header exactly like this (no extra spaces):  
                SERVICE|FILE|ENABLED|PORT|STATUS  
        4.3 After the header, each remaining line must represent one service with fields separated by a literal pipe “|” character in this exact order:  
                <service_name>|<absolute_path_to_ini>|enabled=<value>|port=<value_or_NONE>|<status>  
            • <service_name>          – section name as it appears in the INI file.  
            • <absolute_path_to_ini>  – must match the full path of the file where the service was found.  
            • enabled=<value>         – literally “enabled=true” or “enabled=false”.  
            • port=<value_or_NONE>    – “port=<number>” or the string “port=NONE” if the port key is absent.  
            • <status>                – exactly “OK” or “ERROR” (upper case).  
        4.4 Sort the service lines alphabetically by <service_name> (case-insensitive).  
        4.5 Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline.  

    5.  Verification expectations  
        The automated grader will:  
            • Confirm that /home/user/support_ticket/report/service_audit.log exists.  
            • Validate every line (including header, field order, exact spelling, case, and delimiters).  
            • Cross-check that the STATUS column correctly reflects the rules in step 3b.  
            • Check that alphabetical sorting is correct and that a single newline terminates the file.  

    Remember: you do NOT have root privileges.  Work strictly inside your home directory.  
    When you are finished, double-check permissions so the grader can read the report (normal 0644 is fine).  
    DO NOT delete or move the original INI files.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth used by the automated tests.
        These files are placed in the container BEFORE the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/support_ticket/configs" perms="0755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app1.ini" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
[AuthService]
enabled=true
port=8080

[DataCollector]
enabled=true
; port intentionally omitted
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app2.ini" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
[WebPortal]
enabled=true
port=8080

[Metrics]
enabled=false
port=9100
]]></file>

        <dir path="/home/user/support_ticket/report" perms="0755"/>
    </initial_state>

    <!--
        Expected artefact after successful completion.
    -->
    <expected_output>
        <file path="/home/user/support_ticket/report/service_audit.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
SERVICE|FILE|ENABLED|PORT|STATUS
AuthService|/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app1.ini|enabled=true|port=8080|ERROR
DataCollector|/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app1.ini|enabled=true|port=NONE|ERROR
Metrics|/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app2.ini|enabled=false|port=9100|OK
WebPortal|/home/user/support_ticket/configs/app2.ini|enabled=true|port=8080|ERROR
]]></file>
    </expected_output>
name: task_000000_9da7d327
